title: Kajis mistake lie in episode 15
wikitext:
[]: Kaji's mistake/lie in episode 15             Quote Post by palehorseXIII » Wed May 04, 2011 11:12 am I've been re-watching the series and noticed something I missed the first time that struck me as odd. At the end of episode 15 Misato catches Kaji about to enter Terminal Dogma, they have there little exchange of words and Kaji opens the door revealing Lilith to Misato. However Kaji tells her this is Adam. I thought at first this was a mistake on the part of the subtitles so I looked up the episode on Evageek and discovered this was actually the intended dialog. The page on the episode says that he was either mistaken or lying. Way back in episode 8 Kaji give Gendo Adam in its embryonic state so shouldn't he know this is not Adam? Why would he lie about it? Does he think they may have done something with the Adam to have it reach it's adult form?

[]: Re: Kaji's mistake/lie in episode 15             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed May 04, 2011 11:18 am palehorseXIII wrote: Does he think they may have done something with the Adam to have it reach it's adult form?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed May 04, 2011 11:18 am A couple of threads about this: Kaji has Alzheimers Was Kaji misinformed, or...?

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Wed May 04, 2011 3:17 pm I think it's most likely that the giant in the basement actually was Adam at that point in the series' production.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Wed May 04, 2011 4:49 pm Rodeo wrote: I think it's most likely that the giant in the basement actually was Adam at that point in the series' production.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed May 04, 2011 5:28 pm Rodeo wrote: I think it's most likely that the giant in the basement actually was Adam at that point in the series' production.

[]:             Quote Post by MaskedOmega » Wed May 04, 2011 5:49 pm Maybe to try to give Misato more motivation to betray NERV/SEELE since Adam caused the second impact and they are just keeping it in their basement. I think this might not work though since I cannot remember if they told Misato the truth about the second impact.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Wed May 04, 2011 5:52 pm MaskedOmega wrote: Maybe to try to give Misato more motivation to betray NERV/SEELE since Adam caused the second impact and they are just keeping it in their basement. I think this might not work though since I cannot remember if they told Misato the truth about the second impact.

[]:             Quote Post by MaskedOmega » Wed May 04, 2011 6:12 pm I could not really remember if they told her what really happened since it has been a while since I saw the series. It still works though since when she did discover Adam caused the SI, she remembers that they are keeping it in the basement.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Wed May 04, 2011 7:15 pm liquidus118 wrote: Probably. But that's why we're trying to fanwank around that.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed May 04, 2011 7:20 pm Beautiful analyses Mugwump.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed May 04, 2011 10:13 pm Maybe they just screwed up with the script, it wouldn't be the only occasion of that in the series. Occam's Razor people.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu May 05, 2011 6:01 am "He simply didn't know yet" also works without too much assumptions, tough.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu May 05, 2011 11:35 am This is such a non-issue, because whether or not they intended it to be Adam at that point it the production makes no difference to the STONE COLD FACT that there's a perfectly logical reason why Kaji & Misato would mistakenly assume the being was Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu May 05, 2011 6:19 pm That works too, I just don't understand why we keep making this topic. Maybe we should force all the newbies to read a FAQ before posting in discussions.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu May 05, 2011 7:07 pm Trajan wrote: That works too, I just don't understand why we keep making this topic. Maybe we should force all the newbies to read a FAQ before posting in discussions.
